title: Bag Drill Hook To Low Kick Same Side
summary: Use the lead hook to shift weight and set up the same-side low kick on the bag.
steps: Stand close enough for lead hook range.
Throw lead hook and let the hip rotate.
As the hook retracts, step or sit into lead low kick.
Recover into guard without drifting square.
Alternate sides every five reps.

title: Bag Drill Three Teeps And Angle Out
summary: A bag drill for building teep balance and learning not to stand in front after long-range strikes.
steps: Throw lead teep to the bag.
Throw rear teep to the bag.
Throw lead teep again as the bag returns.
Land and angle out to either side.
Reset before the bag swings back into you.

title: Bag Round Four Count Low Kick Rhythm
summary: A heavy bag rhythm drill to blend hands and low kicks under fatigue.
steps: Start with jab-cross to the bag.
Add rear low kick after the cross.
Reset and throw cross-hook-rear low kick.
Every fourth sequence, teep the bag to manage distance.
Keep the round continuous for three minutes.

title: Catch Body Kick Sweep To Angle Reset
summary: Catch a body kick, off-balance the partner, sweep or dump lightly, then reset at an angle instead of standing square.
steps: Partner throws a controlled body kick.
Catch with elbow tight and ribs covered.
Step slightly outside to avoid absorbing full force.
Pull the caught leg across while sweeping or steering their base leg.
Release safely and reset at an angle.

title: Check Low Kick Return Cross Low Kick
summary: Defend the low kick, land a quick cross as they reset, then return the low kick before they regain stance.
steps: Partner throws a light rear low kick.
Check with the lead shin and keep hands high.
As the kicking leg lands, step down into a right cross.
Immediately return the low kick to their lead leg.
Reset outside their punching line.

title: Clinch Knee Shield To Turn
summary: Use a knee shield inside the clinch to block pressure, then turn the partner as their weight shifts.
steps: Start in light clinch with partner applying forward pressure.
Bring knee shield inside to create space at the hip line.
Keep upper-body frame or collar tie stable.
As partner adjusts, step and turn them to the open side.
Reset with posture before throwing any knee.

title: Clinch Hand Fight To Inside Knee
summary: Fight for inside hand position, stabilize posture, and throw a short inside knee without losing head position.
steps: Start chest-to-chest with both partners light on collar ties.
Swim one arm inside to improve hand position.
Bring hips under the body and keep forehead pressure.
Throw a controlled inside knee to thigh or body line.
Return to base and continue hand fighting.

title: Cross Parry To Outside Angle Combo
summary: Parry the cross, step to the outside angle, and answer with a compact counter before exiting.
steps: Partner feeds jab-cross.
Catch or slip the jab lightly.
Parry the cross inward with a small motion.
Step outside the punching shoulder and throw hook-cross or cross-hook.
Exit before partner squares up.

title: Double Jab To Body Kick On Shell
summary: Draw the guard high with double jab pressure, then punish the covered side with a body kick.
steps: Throw the first jab to measure distance.
Throw the second jab harder to bring the guard up.
As the partner shells, step slightly outside.
Throw the rear body kick into the open side.
Recover with guard high and exit before the counter cross.

title: Elbow Shell Counter On Pocket Entry
summary: Shell against a short punching entry, then answer with a compact elbow as the opponent stays in the pocket.
steps: Partner steps in with light jab-cross or cross-hook.
Shell tight without dropping head blindly.
Feel the second punch land on guard.
Step in half a beat and throw compact elbow to pad or shoulder target.
Frame and exit.

title: Inside Low Kick After Cross Check
summary: Use the cross to bring weight onto the opponent lead leg, then chop the inside low kick before they step out.
steps: Throw a straight right cross down the middle.
Let the cross step land slightly outside.
As their guard reacts, turn the hip into the inside low kick.
Recover the kicking leg under the body.
Reset or follow with jab to prevent the counter.

title: Jab Cross Rear Kick Pad Return
summary: Basic pad sequence that links straight punches to a rear round kick without pausing between hand range and kicking range.
steps: Start in stance with pad holder giving a light forward walk.
Throw jab-cross down the middle.
As the cross retracts, let the rear hip load without stepping too wide.
Throw the rear kick to body-height pad.
Recover the kicking leg under the body and reset guard before moving.

title: Jab Only Sparring With Teep Interrupt
summary: Constraint sparring where both partners use only jabs, with the defender allowed to interrupt forward pressure using the lead teep.
steps: Both partners begin at light contact.
Only jabs are allowed for offense.
If one partner pressures too directly, the other may use a lead teep to the body.
After each teep, both reset distance and continue.
Focus on timing and distance rather than winning the exchange.

title: Lead Knee Off Hand Trap
summary: Trap or pin the lead hand, step in, and throw a lead knee before settling into clinch position.
steps: Partner keeps lead hand active in long guard.
Use rear hand to pin or redirect their lead wrist.
Step in with the rear foot to close distance.
Throw lead knee to body line or belly pad.
Land into frame or collar tie.

title: Long Guard Frame To Knee Entry
summary: Use a long guard frame to stop forward pressure and turn it into a knee entry before the opponent can clinch cleanly.
steps: Partner pressures forward with hands high.
Build a long guard frame across shoulder or collarbone line.
Keep rear hand ready to protect from the hook.
Step the rear foot in and throw a long knee to the body line.
Recover into collar tie, frame, or exit depending on partner reaction.

title: Long Guard To Rear Teep Reset
summary: Use long guard to slow pressure, then rear teep to create a clean reset instead of backing straight up.
steps: Partner walks forward behind light punches.
Build long guard and frame shoulder/chest line.
Take one angled step, not a straight retreat.
Fire rear teep to body line.
Reset stance and guard at long range.

title: Pad Drill Hook Cross Rear Knee
summary: Short pad sequence that uses hook-cross to close the distance for a rear knee.
steps: Pad holder steps in with mitts high.
Throw lead hook to turn the guard.
Throw cross down the middle.
Step the rear foot in and throw rear knee to belly pad.
Pad holder gives light shove so fighter recovers stance.

title: Parry Jab Cross To Rear Knee
summary: Parry the jab, counter cross to occupy the guard, then enter behind the cross with the rear knee.
steps: Partner feeds a light jab.
Parry across with the rear hand without overreaching.
Throw the cross immediately after the parry.
Step in and drive the rear knee to belly pad or body line.
Recover to clinch frame or exit.

title: Partner Drill Feint Teep To Catch Counter
summary: Feint the teep to draw a catch reaction, then punish the reaching hands with a punch or kick counter.
steps: Show the lead teep slowly enough for partner to react.
Partner reaches as if trying to catch.
Retract before contact and plant the foot.
Throw cross, hook, or low kick depending on their reach.
Reset and switch roles.

title: Rear Kick Feint To Step-In Elbow
summary: Show the rear kick to lift the opponent posture or freeze the guard, then step in behind the feint for an elbow entry.
steps: Load the rear hip like a body kick.
Start the kick motion but do not turn fully over.
As the pad holder reacts, step the rear foot down closer.
Throw the step-in horizontal elbow or diagonal elbow to the pad.
Frame or exit immediately after the elbow.

title: Ring Cutting Jab Low Kick Constraint
summary: Constraint sparring where one partner practices cutting space with jab and low kick while the other tries to circle out.
steps: Partner A pressures at light contact.
Partner B circles and exits without throwing heavy counters.
Partner A may use jab, feint, and low kick only.
Goal is to step across the escape path, not chase.
Switch roles every round.

title: Roll Under Hook To Right Kick
summary: Roll under the left hook and answer with a right kick as the partner turns past center.
steps: Partner throws jab-cross-left hook at light speed.
Slip or guard the jab-cross.
Roll under the hook, keeping eyes up.
As you come out, throw the right kick to body or leg.
Exit toward the side you rolled to.

title: Shadowbox Exit After Every Combination
summary: Shadowboxing constraint where every combination must finish with an angle, pivot, frame, or step-out.
steps: Start with any two-to-four strike combination.
After the final strike, immediately exit off the centerline.
Use a pivot, L-step, frame-and-step, or long step back at an angle.
Reset the guard before beginning the next combination.
Repeat for the full round without allowing a straight-back exit.

title: Shadowboxing Counter Rotation Round
summary: A shadowboxing round built around loading counters through rotation instead of throwing arm-only shots.
steps: Start each exchange by imagining an incoming straight punch.
Slip or shoulder turn to load the counter side.
Throw uppercut or hook from the loaded rotation.
Move the feet so the counter does not leave you square.
Finish each sequence with an exit step.

title: Slip Right Step-Through Uppercut Exit
summary: A counter drill for slipping the cross, stepping through into a temporary southpaw shape, firing the left uppercut, then exiting before the return shot.
steps: Partner or pad holder feeds jab-cross at realistic rhythm.
On the cross, slip right and let the right foot step slightly across so the stance turns southpaw.
Keep the head off center and load the left side without standing up.
Drive the left uppercut as the rear foot steps through back toward orthodox.
Exit off-line with a small pivot or angle step instead of backing straight out.

title: Southpaw Open Stance Jab To Rear Body Kick
summary: From southpaw/open stance, jab to occupy the lead hand and land the rear body kick across the open side.
steps: Start in southpaw or switch into southpaw.
Jab to the chest or face line.
Step slightly outside the opponent lead foot.
Throw the rear body kick across the open side.
Recover and exit away from their rear hand.

title: Switch Step Lead Kick Off Jab
summary: Use the jab to hide the switch step and land the lead body kick without telegraphing.
steps: Throw a relaxed jab while staying tall.
As the jab retracts, switch the feet quietly.
Turn the hip over into the lead body kick.
Land balanced and bring hands back to guard.
Repeat with occasional fake switch to keep rhythm honest.

title: Teep Entry To Right Cross
summary: Use the lead teep to freeze or move the opponent, then step in behind the retracting leg to land the cross.
steps: Show the lead teep from long range.
Touch the body or hip with the teep, focusing on balance over power.
Retract the leg straight back under the hip.
As the opponent settles, step in and throw the right cross.
Exit to the lead side or follow with left hook if the pad holder stays shelled.

title: Wall Drill Posture Break And Turn
summary: Use the wall as a constraint to practice breaking posture and turning the partner without muscling the clinch.
steps: Partner starts with back near wall or ropes.
Establish collar tie and bicep control.
Use a small pull-and-step to break posture.
Turn the partner off the wall by stepping across their base.
Reset and switch roles.

title: Technical Defensive Mirror Round
summary: A mirror-style technical drill where one person leads defensive movement and the other copies to sharpen footwork and head movement.
steps: Partner A leads with slips, rolls, pivots, and small exits.
Partner B mirrors the movement without throwing strikes.
Switch leader every thirty seconds.
Keep stance width and guard consistent.
Add light jab feints in the final minute.

title: Technical Line Drill Teep To Pivot
summary: A simple technical line drill for teeping, landing balanced, and pivoting off the centerline.
steps: Move forward in stance across the mat.
Throw lead teep every three steps.
Retract the teep and land softly.
Pivot off the lead foot as if avoiding the counter.
Continue down the line and switch directions back.
